addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Check directory sizes in human readable format

content.text:
```
du -sh FROM_BLUE--1 FROM_BLUE--2 FROM_BLUE--3 FROM_SEAGATE_3TB--1 FROM_SEAGATE_3TB--2 FROM_SEAGATE_3TB--3 FROM_SILVER--1 FROM_SILVER--2 FROM_SILVER--3
```

Or more concisely with a glob:

```
du -sh FROM_*
```

message.content: I am copying from one synology NAS to another.  One is CIFS mounted to the other.  I seem to have to kick off the copy in the webconsole.  Will the copy run to completion even if I close the webconsole?

content.text:
Short answer: **no, not by default.** A command launched in the Synology DSM web terminal (or a regular SSH session) is tied to that shell — closing the browser tab usually kills the shell, and the copy dies with it (SIGHUP).

To make it survive, detach it from the shell. Options, best to worst for your case:

**`screen` or `tmux`** (best — you can reattach and watch progress):
```
screen -S copy
# run your cp/rsync here
# detach: Ctrl-A then D
```
Reattach later with `screen -r copy`. Synology has `screen` available via the package manager or entware; `tmux` similarly.

**`nohup` + background** (simplest, fire-and-forget):
```
nohup rsync -aHAX --info=progress2 /src/ /dst/ > ~/copy.log 2>&1 &
```
Then `tail -f ~/copy.log` to monitor. Closing the console won't kill it.

**`disown`** (if you already started it):
```
Ctrl-Z
bg
disown -h %1
```

Side note: prefer `rsync -aHAX` over `cp` for NAS-to-NAS — preserves hardlinks/ACLs/xattrs and is restartable if it dies mid-run.

Web search results for query: "Synology DSM web terminal close browser does command keep running background"

Links: [{"title":"Background Tasks when browser is closed","url":"https://community.synology.com/enu/forum/1/post/185983"},{"title":"Synology: Basic Command Lines For DSM 7 – Marius Hosting","url":"https://mariushosting.com/synology-basic-command-lines-for-dsm-7/"},{"title":"Terminal | DSM - Synology Knowledge Center","url":"https://kb.synology.com/en-us/DSM/help/DSM/AdminCenter/system_terminal"},{"title":"Run command in background on a Synology NAS with nohup - $bLOG","url":"https://blog.fotto.de/run-command-in-background-on-a-synology-nas/"},{"title":"How to follow background jobs - Synology Forum","url":"https://forum.synology.com/enu/viewtopic.php?t=94824"},{"title":"Web Interface and effect of closing web browser | SynoForum.com - The Unofficial Synology Forum","url":"https://www.synoforum.com/threads/web-interface-and-effect-of-closing-web-browser.14053/"},{"title":"DSM7 UI closes open app? | SynoForum.com - The Unofficial Synology Forum","url":"https://www.synoforum.com/threads/dsm7-ui-closes-open-app.7479/"},{"title":"Terminal windows inside DSM?","url":"https://community.synology.com/enu/forum/1/post/133977"},{"title":"DSM Web browser and terminal - Synology Forum","url":"https://forum.synology.com/enu/viewtopic.php?t=42155"},{"title":"Background process kill after logoff/ shutting putty.","url":"https://community.synology.com/enu/forum/17/post/28288"}]

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about running commands in the background when closing the browser in Synology DSM:

**For Web Terminal (DSM built-in):**
When you close the browser or logoff, tasks go to background. Or when you click "hide".

**For SSH Terminal (more reliable for background processes):**
To run a program that doesn't quit if you close the ssh session use nohup (no hangup). Attention, you have to run it as root for it to work! The basic syntax would be: `nohup <command> &`

**Key Difference:**
If you're using the web-based Terminal within DSM itself and simply close the browser, the behavior depends on the type of operation. Built-in DSM operations work in the background as DSM processes, but for arbitrary commands run in the terminal, using `nohup` ensures they continue running after the session ends.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.prompt: How to keep commands running in background on Synology after closing browser/SSH? What about the DSM Terminal app specifically?

content.content:
# Running Commands in Background on Synology

Based on the article provided, the standard approach uses `nohup`:

**The Method:**
First, gain root access, then use nohup with the ampersand to background the process:

```
sudo su
nohup <command> &
```

The article emphasizes that "you have to run it as root for it to work!" An example given is:
`nohup cat /dev/zero | split -b 4095m - /volumeUSB2/usbshare/zeros -d --additional-suffix=.file &`

**How It Works:**
The nohup utility prevents processes from terminating when you close your SSH session, allowing long-running tasks to continue uninterrupted in the background.

**Regarding DSM Terminal:**
The provided content doesn't specifically address the DSM Terminal app or any differences in how it handles background processes compared to standard SSH sessions. The technique shown would apply to SSH access, but you'd need additional documentation for DSM Terminal-specific behavior.

input.query: Synology File Station copy continues background close browser window

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Synology File Station copy continues background close browser window"

Links: [{"title":"Background copy bug in Filestation","url":"https://community.synology.com/enu/forum/17/post/6734"},{"title":"Background Tasks when browser is closed","url":"https://community.synology.com/enu/forum/1/post/185983"},{"title":"copy completion with filestation - Synology Forum","url":"https://forum.synology.com/enu/viewtopic.php?t=22014"},{"title":"Web Interface and effect of closing web browser | SynoForum.com - The Unofficial Synology Forum","url":"https://www.synoforum.com/threads/web-interface-and-effect-of-closing-web-browser.14053/"},{"title":"File transfer not showing up in background tasks - Synology Forum","url":"https://forum.synology.com/enu/viewtopic.php?t=75343"},{"title":"Need the ability to perform file operations in the background · Issue #3767 · filebrowser/filebrowser","url":"https://github.com/filebrowser/filebrowser/issues/3767"},{"title":"Manage File Station Settings | File Station - Synology Knowledge Center","url":"https://kb.synology.com/en-us/DSM/help/FileStation/settings?version=7"},{"title":"Background Tasks Details - Synology Forum","url":"https://forum.synology.com/enu/viewtopic.php?t=97749"},{"title":"background task icon gone in task bar - Synology Forum","url":"https://forum.synology.com/enu/viewtopic.php?t=85428"},{"title":"How to follow background jobs - Synology Forum","url":"https://forum.synology.com/enu/viewtopic.php?t=94824"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Synology File Station copy operations continuing in the background when closing the browser window:

## Key Information

When you start a file copy using File Station and close the browser, it continues in the background. The copy goes to background when you close the browser or logoff.

## Viewing Progress After Closing

When you "Hide" a copy/move process, "Background Tasks" appears and you will have "All" that will pop-up the window where you can track the copy/move progress. Background Tasks is on the left side of "Notifications".

## Known Issues

The search results reveal some long-standing issues with background task visibility:

- After closing the transfer status window, or after logging out and logging back in, there is no way to view the status of or cancel the transfer
- A background task gets created and appears on the task bar, but after some time it disappears, even though the background copy task still runs
- The copy stays in background tasks and File station doesn't show the copy transfer anymore

If the background task icon disappears, one workaround is to logout of the DSM web console and log back in, which will make the Background Task Icon reappear.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Yes — **File Station copies run server-side and survive the browser closing.** The browser is just a UI; the actual copy job runs as a DSM task on the NAS. Closing Chrome (or even logging out of DSM) doesn't kill it.

How to verify and monitor:

- After closing/reopening the browser, log back into DSM — the copy shows up under **Background Tasks** (icon next to Notifications, top-right of the DSM desktop).
- If you click "Hide" on the transfer dialog while it's running, it moves to Background Tasks.

**Caveats from the field:**
- There's a long-standing DSM quirk where the Background Tasks icon sometimes disappears from the taskbar even though the copy is still running. Workaround: log out of DSM and log back in — the icon reappears.
- A handful of users have reported the transfer status becoming uncancellable / unviewable after closing the dialog. The copy still proceeds; you just lose UI visibility until reload.
- This is **only** true for File Station. If you're running `cp`/`rsync` inside the **DSM Terminal app** (the in-browser SSH window), that *does* die when you close the tab — same SIGHUP problem as SSH. Use `nohup`/`screen` there.

For a NAS-to-NAS over CIFS, File Station is fine, but `rsync` over SSH (with `nohup` or `screen`) is more robust — it's resumable, preserves attributes better, and gives you real progress.

Sources:
- [Web Interface and effect of closing web browser — SynoForum](https://www.synoforum.com/threads/web-interface-and-effect-of-closing-web-browser.14053/)
- [Background Tasks when browser is closed — Synology Community](https://community.synology.com/enu/forum/1/post/185983)
- [Background task icon gone in task bar — Synology Forum](https://forum.synology.com/enu/viewtopic.php?t=85428)
- [File transfer not showing up in background tasks — Synology Forum](https://forum.synology.com/enu/viewtopic.php?t=75343)
- [Run command in background on a Synology NAS with nohup](https://blog.fotto.de/run-command-in-background-on-a-synology-nas/)

content: You're copying between two Synology NASes over CIFS and asked whether closing the DSM web console kills the job. Answer: File Station copies keep running server-side, but Terminal-app commands die unless wrapped in nohup or screen. (disable recaps in /config)
